title: hooks: Allow local control of OCI stages via extensionStages
body:
This allows callers to avoid delegating to OCI runtimes for cases where they feel that the runtime hook handling is unreliable (e.g. see [here][1] and the following few comments).  If/when this lands, I'll work up a PR that sets `poststop` as an extension stage in `libpod/container_internal.go` and then invokes those hooks after deletion.

CC @mheon.

[1]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/730#issuecomment-392959938

Besides the Go issues above, [the `cloud Podman` results][1] have same Python failures we're seing in #856.

[1]: https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/0ad15bbaabcc49f02043e81f7f88faea343bd815.2.1527714339367363446/output.log
LGTM.  Tests passing except cloud Podman which seems to be an unrelated network issue happening in a number of PRs.
My only concern about this would be the hooks getting called twice with the posthook, will this cause issues.

:pushpin: Commit 0ad15bb has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 0ad15bbaabcc49f02043e81f7f88faea343bd815 -->

> My only concern about this would be the hooks getting called twice with the posthook, will this cause issues.

If a caller lists `poststop` (say) [as an extension stage][1], it will be [in `extensionStageHooks`][2] but [not in the OCI config][3].  So you'll only get double calls if libpod takes control of a stage and then double-calls itself.  The runtime will never hear about those hooks.

[1]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/4dfe0d60f3593360b9d43d8dd38981e971c9b642/pkg/hooks/hooks_test.go#L196
[2]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/4dfe0d60f3593360b9d43d8dd38981e971c9b642/pkg/hooks/hooks_test.go#L214
[3]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/4dfe0d60f3593360b9d43d8dd38981e971c9b642/pkg/hooks/hooks_test.go#L205-L211
